question.text: Under SEBI IA Regulations, change in control of an investment adviser requires:

answer.text: Intimation to SEBI within 30 days of the change
Prior approval from SEBI before the change takes effect
Approval from the recognized supervisory body only
No regulatory approval if the new controlling entity is also SEBI-registered

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI IA Regulations explicitly require prior approval from SEBI for any change in control of the investment adviser — this is a pre-condition, not a post-event notification. **The Trap:** 'Prior approval' is different from 'intimation within 30 days' — the change cannot happen without SEBI's advance approval. **Cross-Exam Context:** Change in control provisions are tested across Series IX (Merchant Banking) and Series III-A (Intermediary Compliance) as they apply to all SEBI-registered intermediaries. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Plan for regulatory timelines in any M&A involving an IA entity — SEBI approval can take several months and the transaction cannot close without it.
